name: skeleton
title: Skeleton
description: A loading placeholder that mirrors the geometry of not-yet-loaded content. Text, block, and circle shapes with a motion-safe pulse (static block under reduced motion). Always aria-hidden — it conveys no progress or content; announce loading on a surrounding role=status / aria-busy container or via the optional sr-only label.
useWhen: Filling the space of content with a stable, known layout while it loads (cards, list rows, avatars, text lines)
Reducing perceived load time and layout shift by reserving the final geometry up front
Showing a first-load placeholder for a region whose shape you can predict
avoidWhen: An indeterminate, spinner-style wait with no known content shape (use a Spinner)
A determinate operation with measurable progress (use a Progress bar)
Conveying that the load itself is the message to a screen reader (put role=status / aria-busy on the container instead — the skeleton is aria-hidden)
Content whose final size is unknown, where a placeholder would cause layout shift on arrival

keyboard: The skeleton is not interactive and never receives focus
It carries no keyboard behavior of its own
notes: The placeholder is always aria-hidden and exposes no role, name, or progress semantics to assistive technology.

Announce the loading state on a surrounding container with role=status (or aria-busy=true), present in the DOM before content is injected; the optional label prop renders an sr-only role=status for a lone placeholder.
The pulse animation is gated behind motion-safe; prefers-reduced-motion users see a static block, satisfying WCAG 2.3.3.
bg-muted is a theme token, so the placeholder remains visible in forced-colors mode.
